architecture.summary: Codec is a constrained decoding system for cross-lingual label projection. It translates source sentences without annotation markers to a target language via a machine translation model (NLLB), then uses a specialized constrained decoding algorithm to inject span markers at correct positions in the translation template. The algorithm operates in three stages: (1) pruning unlikely opening-marker positions by comparing conditional token probabilities with/without markers, (2) branch-and-bound depth-first search to find top-k hypotheses that satisfy constraints (exact marker count, fixed template text), and (3) re-ranking using hypothesis-level (generative log-probability) and span-level (source-to-target span translation probability) scores. Information flows from source text through MT without markers, then through constrained decoding that enforces template fidelity and marker count while selecting the best projection.

biblio.title: Constrained Decoding for Cross-lingual Label Projection

claims.statement: On average, Codec outperforms EasyProject by a large margin in translate-train setting on MasakhaNER2.0, with improvements of at least 1.5 F1 in nine languages
In translate-test setting on MasakhaNER2.0, Codec achieves average F1 of 70.4, outperforming Awes-align (65.8)
For chiShona in translate-train NER, Codec achieves +16.5 F1 improvement over EasyProject
For Bambara in translate-test NER, Codec is approximately 60 times faster than exact branch-and-bound search while losing only 0.6 absolute F1
On ACE-2005 Event Argument Extraction in translate-train setting, Codec achieves average F1 of 53.8, outperforming EasyProject (52.6) and Awes-align (52.8)
The heuristic lower bound (Eq. 7-8) with α=3 achieves roughly the same F1 as exact search with re-ranking while having much better decoding speed
In Chinese manual analysis, more than 60% of Codec errors occur because the correct hypothesis is in the top-k found by Codec, but the re-ranking step fails to retrieve it
Codec is applicable to both translate-train and translate-test settings, whereas EasyProject is not applicable to translate-test due to its marker-based approach
